Title: Exploitation of Host-Derived N-Feruloylputrescine by Fusarium graminearum Promotes DON Biosynthesis and Wheat Rachis Colonisation

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Progressive induction of PHT3 homologous genes during Fusarium graminearum infection is associated with activation of putrescine-derived phenolamide biosynthesis in wheat.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A BLASTP search using rice PHT3 identified 15 PHT3 homologues in wheat. 2. These genes were barely expressed in uninfected wheat heads but were progressively induced at 24, 48, and 72 h after F. graminearum inoculation. 3. WheatOmics data showed that PHT3 homologues were also strongly induced by flg22 and responsive to chitin, supporting a role in pathogen-responsive phenolamide biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Barely expressed in uninfected heads; induced at 24–72 h after F. graminearum infection; induced by flg22 and chitin
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Barely expressed in uninfected heads; induced at 24–72 h after F. graminearum infection; induced by flg22 and chitin
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BLASTP homology identification; infection time-course expression profiling; WheatOmics expression analysis under flg22 and chitin treatment
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fusarium graminearum infection; flg22 treatment; chitin treatment
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Untargeted metabolomics by UPLC-MS/MS; WheatOmics expression data (accession NA)
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper treats PHT3 homologues as key biosynthetic genes for putrescine-derived phenolamides in wheat, but no single wheat PHT3 gene was functionally dissected by transgenics or enzyme assay in this study.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Context-dependent defence metabolite: associated with resistance to stripe rust, powdery mildew and rice blast, but can be exploited by Fusarium graminearum to enhance virulence through DON induction.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Induction of phenolamide biosynthesis may strengthen defence against some foliar pathogens while increasing susceptibility to Fusarium head blight through metabolite exploitation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Phenolamide pathway engineering should be deployed cautiously because elevating products such as Ferput may improve resistance to some pathogens but worsen Fusarium head blight in wheat.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Rapid induction of AHT1 homologous genes during Fusarium graminearum infection is associated with activation of agmatine-derived phenolamide biosynthesis in wheat.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Fourteen AHT1 homologous genes were identified in wheat. 2. Their expression was rapidly induced at 24 hpi after F. graminearum inoculation and remained stable at later infection stages. 3. AHT1 homologues also responded to flg22 and chitin, consistent with involvement in pathogen-responsive phenolamide biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low/basal in uninfected tissue; rapidly induced at 24 hpi and maintained at 48–72 hpi; responsive to flg22 and chitin
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Low/basal in uninfected tissue; rapidly induced at 24 hpi and maintained at 48–72 hpi; responsive to flg22 and chitin
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BLASTP homology identification; infection time-course expression profiling; WheatOmics expression analysis under flg22 and chitin treatment
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fusarium graminearum infection; flg22 treatment; chitin treatment
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Untargeted metabolomics by UPLC-MS/MS; WheatOmics expression data (accession NA)
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: These genes were analyzed as a homologous group; the study did not functionally validate an individual wheat AHT1 member by enzyme assay or transgenics.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phenolamides function as defence-associated metabolites with pathogen-dependent beneficial or detrimental outcomes.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhancing this pathway may boost defence-associated phenolamide accumulation but could create metabolites exploitable by Fusarium graminearum.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as markers of inducible phenolamide pathway activation, but pathway enhancement may require balancing against DON-promoting outcomes.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Virulence factor required for efficient rachis colonisation and spikelet-to-spikelet spread in wheat
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: In infected wheat kernels and fungal cultures
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxygenation steps in trichothecene biosynthesis associated with DON production.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Oxygenated trichothecene intermediate(s) leading to DON
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for biosynthesis of the target end product DON
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key biosynthetic enzyme; used here as a marker for DON biosynthesis and toxisome formation, but rate-limiting status was not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Ferput upregulated TRI1 expression and enhanced Tri1p-labeled toxisome formation, consistent with increased DON biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TRI1 transcript abundance increased when Ferput was supplied as the sole nitrogen source compared with putrescine. 2. A TRI1-GFP transformant showed stronger toxisome formation under Ferput treatment. 3. These changes coincided with substantially elevated DON production.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in DON-inducing LTB medium containing Ferput relative to putrescine
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in DON-inducing LTB medium containing Ferput relative to putrescine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; TRI1-GFP imaging of toxisomes; DON quantification in culture and infected kernels
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ferput supplied as sole nitrogen source in LTB medium; wheat infection with exogenous Ferput pretreatment
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Tri1p fluorescent tagging was specifically used to visualize toxisome assembly, linking Ferput to DON-associated cellular differentiation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Targeting fungal DON pathway components such as TRI1 could help block pathogen exploitation of host phenolamides and reduce Fusarium head blight virulence.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Virulence factor promoting wheat rachis colonisation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: In fungal culture and infected wheat kernels
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes farnesyl diphosphate to trichodiene, the first committed step of trichothecene biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms the first committed precursor of DON
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Ferput increased TRI5 transcript levels, supporting stimulation of DON biosynthetic flux.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TRI5 was strongly upregulated by Ferput relative to putrescine in DON-inducing medium. 2. Elevated TRI5 expression accompanied increased DON accumulation. 3. The data support Ferput as a strong inducer of fungal DON biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in LTB medium with Ferput as sole nitrogen source
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in LTB medium with Ferput as sole nitrogen source
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ferput treatment in DON-inducing LTB medium
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study measured transcriptional response rather than direct enzyme activity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: TRI5 remains a prime antifungal target to suppress DON contamination under host metabolite-inducing conditions.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive regulation of TRI gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Ferput treatment increased TRI10 transcript abundance, associated with activation of TRI1, TRI5, TRI12 and elevated DON production
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Ferput upregulated TRI10, consistent with transcriptional activation of the DON biosynthetic program.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TRI10 expression was markedly increased under Ferput compared with putrescine. 2. Increased TRI10 expression coincided with increased expression of core TRI genes and higher DON accumulation. 3. These findings support activation of the DON regulatory network by host-derived Ferput.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Acts as a core regulatory component of the TRI cluster whose induction reflects activation of DON biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in LTB medium with Ferput as sole nitrogen source
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in LTB medium with Ferput as sole nitrogen source
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ferput as sole nitrogen source in LTB medium
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper did not directly dissect TRI10 mechanism, but used its induction as evidence that Ferput activates the DON regulatory program.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Virulence factor for rachis colonisation in wheat
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: In fungal culture and infected wheat kernels
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Interfering with TRI10-mediated induction could reduce fungal responsiveness to host metabolites that stimulate DON biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Trichothecene efflux transporter / pathway-associated regulator-like component
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Pathway-associated transport support for DON production/handling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Ferput increased TRI12 transcript levels along with other TRI genes during enhanced DON production.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TRI12 expression was markedly upregulated by Ferput compared with putrescine. 2. This induction accompanied increased DON accumulation in culture. 3. The result supports broad activation of the TRI cluster by host-derived Ferput.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Serves as a core TRI-cluster component whose induction tracks strong activation of the DON production program.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in LTB medium with Ferput as sole nitrogen source
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in LTB medium with Ferput as sole nitrogen source
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ferput as sole nitrogen source in LTB medium
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TRI12 is functionally better categorized as a transporter rather than a transcriptional regulator, but under the provided classification scheme it is included as a regulation-associated core gene because it was among the four core TRI genes experimentally examined for the DON response.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Virulence factor associated with Fusarium spread in wheat through DON production
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: In fungal culture and infected wheat kernels
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for suppressing fungal toxin export/tolerance under DON-inducing host environments.
